prompt: Create an ultra-realistic accidental candid photo shot on a low-end Android phone inside a cramped, old, messy kitchen at night. The image should feel like a badly timed snapshot: tilted framing, harsh warm indoor lighting, high ISO noise, compression artifacts, imperfect autofocus, motion blur, and a large out-of-focus finger partially covering the bottom half of the lens. In the center-right, show an older woman wearing a burgundy floral short-sleeve house dress, caught mid-motion as she aggressively swings a slipper or sandal toward the camera; the swung object is very close to the lens on the left side and heavily motion-blurred. Her face is obscured by a plain rectangular blur/censor block, while her body remains visible. The background is a worn domestic kitchen with stained beige walls, peeling paint, a dark wooden wall cabinet, an old window or door frame on the left, a rice cooker, stacked pots, plastic containers, a red bucket, cluttered shelves, and scattered household items. Make the composition awkward and imperfect, as if the photographer panicked and accidentally captured the moment, with the camera slightly rotated, uneven exposure, soft focus, dirty textures, and raw documentary realism. Use vertical smartphone aspect ratio 9:16.
